name: Cosmotop System Monitor MCP
what_it_does: Cosmotop MCP server enabling AI agents to monitor local system resources — querying CPU usage, memory consumption, disk I/O, running processes, and system health metrics, bringing system monitoring capabilities similar to htop/btop into agent-driven infrastructure and debugging workflows.
use_cases: Checking CPU and memory usage from resource monitoring agents
Identifying resource-hungry processes from performance debugging agents
Monitoring disk usage and I/O from capacity planning agents
Tracking system health metrics from infrastructure monitoring agents
Diagnosing performance bottlenecks from troubleshooting agents
Automating system resource reports from DevOps agents
not_for: Production infrastructure monitoring (use Prometheus, Datadog for prod)
Historical metrics and alerting (use dedicated monitoring stacks)
best_when: An agent needs real-time local system resource data — CPU, memory, processes — for debugging, performance analysis, or local infrastructure awareness.
avoid_when: You need remote server monitoring, historical metrics, or alerting — use Prometheus, Datadog, or Grafana MCPs instead.

auth.notes: No authentication — reads local system metrics via OS APIs. Runs with user-level privileges.

pricing.notes: Free, open source community MCP. Self-hosted locally.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no rate limits; polling frequency determines overhead

known_agent_gotchas: Local-only — cannot monitor remote systems via this MCP
Privileged process info may be unavailable without elevated permissions
Community MCP — metric naming may differ from industry standards
High polling frequency can itself consume resources — use sparingly
Process names and PIDs can change between calls — handle transient state
macOS and Linux may return different metric structures
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local OS metric access. No credentials. No network access. Community MCP. Be cautious about exposing process lists to untrusted agents — process names may reveal sensitive services.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local metrics only. No auth needed. No secrets. Community MCP. Process list exposure is informational risk.
